name: City tour Bilbao – circular through the old town, Museo Guggenheim and San Mamés
name_original: City tour Bilbao – circular por el casco antiguo, Museo Guggenheim y San Mamés
region_intro: Touring cycling around Bilbao offers diverse landscapes, from coastal routes along the Bay of Biscay to paths winding through green hills and along the Nervión River. The region features a mix of gentle riverside trails, undulating countryside, and more challenging ascents into the surrounding mountains. This geographical variety provides a range of terrains suitable for different cycling preferences. Best touring cycling routes around Bilbao The most popular touring cycling route is City tour Bilbao – circular through the old town, Museo Guggenheim and San Mamés, an 11.4 miles (18.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour 21 minutes to complete. This route offers a circular exploration of Bilbao's urban landmarks. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Vizcaya Bridge – Guggenheim Museum Bilbao loop from Bilbao La Concordia, a moderate 21.1 miles (34.0 km) path. This loop connects the historic Vizcaya Bridge with the modern Guggenheim Museum. Local touring cyclists also love the Riverside Cycle Path to Bilbao – Superb bike trail loop from Olabeaga, a 28.2 miles (45.3 km) trail leading through riverside paths, often completed in about 2 hours 47 minutes. Touring cycling around Bilbao is defined by coastal paths, green hills, and the Nervión River estuary. The network offers options for different ability levels, from urban loops to longer countryside explorations. The routes in Bilbao are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 260 reviews. More than 4000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Bilbao's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Bilbao?
A: There are over 60 touring cycling routes available around Bilbao, offering a wide variety of experiences. These include everything from easy riverside paths to more challenging mountain ascents.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when touring cycling around Bilbao?
A: Bilbao offers a diverse landscape for touring cyclists. You can expect scenic coastal routes along the Bay of Biscay, gentle paths winding along the Nervión River, and routes through green hills and valleys. For more experienced riders, there are also challenging ascents into the surrounding mountains, such as Mount Artxanda, offering panoramic views.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Bilbao?
A: The region generally offers pleasant cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are often ideal, with milder temperatures and less rainfall. Summer can also be enjoyable, especially along the coast, though it can be warmer. Winter is typically cooler and wetter, but many routes remain accessible.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Bilbao?
A: Yes, Bilbao has several easy touring cycling routes perfect for beginners. Many of the riverside paths, like those along the Nervión River, offer flat and accessible rides. An example of a moderate route that is popular and manageable is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1065687823/city-tour-bilbao-circular-through-the-old-town-museo-guggenheim-and-san-mames" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;City tour Bilbao – circular through the old town, Museo Guggenheim and San Mamés&lt;/a&gt;, which explores urban landmarks.

Q: Can I find challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders in Bilbao?
A: Absolutely. For experienced riders seeking a challenge, Bilbao's surrounding green mountains provide ample opportunities. Routes that venture into areas like Urkiola Natural Park or involve climbs up Mount Artxanda offer significant elevation gains and rewarding panoramic views. There are 36 difficult routes available for those looking for a more strenuous ride.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling routes in Bilbao?
A: Yes, many of the flatter, dedicated bike paths along the Nervión River and coastal areas are suitable for families. These routes often avoid heavy traffic and provide pleasant scenery. The ride from Bilbao to Getxo's Old Port is a popular, beginner-friendly option that families can enjoy.

Q: What interesting landmarks and attractions can I see along touring cycling routes in Bilbao?
A: Many routes in Bilbao pass by significant landmarks and attractions. You can cycle past the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/335425" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guggenheim Museum&lt;/a&gt;, cross the modern &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2911339" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zubizuri Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, or visit the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3902698" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vizcaya Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, a UNESCO World Heritage site. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24901404" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vizcaya Bridge – Guggenheim Museum Bilbao loop from Bilbao La Concordia&lt;/a&gt; is a great route to see both.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes available in Bilbao?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes around Bilbao are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38288914" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riverside Cycle Path to Bilbao – Superb bike trail loop from Olabeaga&lt;/a&gt; offers a substantial circular ride along the river.

Q: Are touring cycling routes in Bilbao dog-friendly?
A: While specific routes aren't designated 'dog-friendly,' many of the riverside paths and less crowded countryside trails are suitable for cycling with a well-behaved dog on a leash, or in a bike trailer. Always ensure your dog is comfortable and safe, and be mindful of local regulations regarding pets in public areas.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes that pass by cafes or places to eat?
A: Yes, many routes, especially those that pass through towns and villages or along the city's waterfront, offer opportunities to stop for refreshments. The vibrant 'pintxos' culture means you'll often find cafes and taverns in places like Getxo's Old Port or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/643283" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza Nueva (Bilbao)&lt;/a&gt;, perfect for a break.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Bilbao?
A: The touring cycling routes in Bilbao are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 260 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse scenery, from urban landmarks to coastal views, and the well-maintained paths that make exploring the region enjoyable.

Q: Can I reach touring cycling trailheads using public transport in Bilbao?
A: Bilbao has a good public transport network, including metro, tram, and bus services, which can often get you close to various trailheads. The city is bike-friendly, and some public transport options may accommodate bicycles, though it's always best to check specific rules and times, especially during peak hours.

Q: Are there any routes that explore the industrial heritage of the region?
A: Yes, some routes, particularly those venturing into the Enkarterri - Meatzaldea area, combine mountainous terrain with ancient mining sites. These paths allow cyclists to experience the region's industrial past alongside its natural beauty, offering a unique historical perspective.
tips:
Paseo Uribitarte 48011 Bilbao
Located in the Paseo de Uribitarte, and consisting of four statues, the sculpture ‘Las Sirgueras’, by artist Dora Salazar, is a recognition of the importance of women's work and the path to equality.  The work pays tribute to the figure of the sirgueras (the rope girls), women who towed vessels along the estuary using only a rope and their own strength.
https://www.bilbaoturismo.net/BilbaoTurismo/en/outdoor-art/sirgueras_2
The Guggenheim museum not only cleaned the face of the dirty Bilbao of the 80's but also gave the city a modern and avant-garde air. A 'must' to visit in this city.

Opened in 1997, it facilitates access to the Deusto district from Plaça del Sagrado Corazón. Its most emblematic element is a 45-meter-high lighting tower. This viaduct, which allows the transit of vehicles and pedestrians and has bike lanes, is used daily by about 40,000 vehicles. Its name is taken from the old shipyards located in the same place and closed at the end of the 80s, a name that it also shares with the Euskalduna Conference and Music Palace located at one end.
https://www.bilbaoturismo.net/BilbaoTurismo/es/nuevos-puentes/puente-euskalduna
Views of Bilbao from the Artxanda viewpoint

It was inaugurated in 1756, according to a project by Ignacio de Ibero, and it draws attention to the showiness of its baroque façade crowned by two towers, a meeting point for sailors before going to sea.
It is said that in 1812 the temple was chosen by the General Assemblies of Bizkaia to sign the liberal Constitution of Cádiz.
The exterior and interior restoration has allowed this church to recover its splendor, eliminating additions and showing again the quality of its ashlar stone factory of Ganguren (Galdakao).
Its interior, an octagon within a square, houses altarpieces and sculptures by Juan de Mena.
https://www.destinoseuskadi.com/directorio/iglesia-san-nicolas-de-bari/
The old town of Bilbao is a very charming place as it recalls the entrails and industry of old Bilbao as well as the most traditional Basque shops. A nice walk to do both with your bike and on foot.

The enormously beautiful theatre building with Renaissance elements is named after the musician and composer Juan Crisostomo de Arriaga, who in his time was also known as the Spanish Mozart. Arriaga wrote his first opera at the age of 13 and died of tuberculosis shortly before his 20th birthday. The Arriaga theatre was rebuilt in 1985 after the severe damage from a severe flood in August 1983.
